steps.title: Apply cofunction identities
steps.content: Use the cofunction shortcut: $\cos(90 - a)° = \sin a°$ and $\sin(90 - b)° = \cos b°$. With $y = 0$, $\sin(a + 0)° = \sin a°$. The expression becomes: $$2\sin a° \cdot \cos b° + \sin a° \cdot \cos b°.$$

steps.title: Factor and substitute
steps.content: Factor: $3\sin a° \cdot \cos b°$. Substitute $\sin a° = 0.50$ and $\cos b° = 0.99$: $$3 \times 0.50 \times 0.99 = 1.485.$$ That matches choice 1.485 — 1.485 is correct.
